key_insight: SimPhysNet predicts laser-weld penetration state accurately by combining physics-informed self-supervised feature learning with few-shot classification, reducing dependence on large labeled datasets.
engineering_relevance: This enables more practical, data-efficient monitoring and quality control for automated laser welding, especially where labeled defect data are scarce.
method: Use a PINN-guided contrastive self-supervised backbone to learn physically meaningful molten-pool and keyhole features from unlabeled images.
Apply three image augmentation tasks to improve representation robustness and generalization.
Use prototypical-network few-shot learning to classify penetration state from only a small labeled set.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 96.06% classification accuracy using only 200 labeled images, about 5% of the labeled dataset, comparable to fully supervised methods.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and representativeness of the unlabeled welding images and on how well the learned physics priors transfer across laser-welding conditions.
paper_title: A welding penetration prediction model for laser welding process based on self-supervised learning using physics-informed neural networks
